reasoning:
The resolution criteria specify an "IF AND ONLY IF" (IFF) condition between Trump winning the Nobel Peace Prize and a ceasefire in Ukraine occurring before 2026. The prompt explicitly notes that the Nobel market has already resolved "No." Therefore, for this market to resolve "Yes," there must NOT be a ceasefire in Ukraine before 2026 (Both No = Yes).

Based on evidence up to late October 2025, the likelihood of a bilateral ceasefire being signed and taking effect before 2026 is extremely low. Following a mid-October push for a ceasefire by President Trump, Moscow formally rejected the demand for an immediate ceasefire on October 21-22, resulting in the planned Budapest summit being put on hold. The war remains a "brutal stalemate," with Russian forces prioritizing ongoing territorial offensives in Donetsk. Since previous market probability was around 91% on October 16, and peace talks have subsequently broken down, the probability of a ceasefire before the end of the year has substantially decreased. Consequently, the probability of this market resolving YES (meaning NO ceasefire) is very high.
